claims.quote:
Bone Marrow Transplant Elective
We offer an elective for medical students on bone marrow transplantation and its role in treating hematologic diseases.
claims.claimText: MSK offers a Bone Marrow Transplant Elective for medical students focused on BMT and its role in treating hematologic diseases.
claims.limitations: sourceScope is UNKNOWN_SCOPE per packet; cannot promote to PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE without scope confirmation. Audience (US MD/DO vs IMG) is not stated.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Quote explicitly states an elective for medical students — pre-residency USCE-relevant content.

claims.quote: Medical Student Elective Program: How to Apply
claims.claimText: MSK references a Medical Student Elective Program with a 'How to Apply' pathway.
claims.limitations: Linked label only; application details, eligibility, fees and durations are not stated on this page. UNKNOWN_SCOPE prevents public-safe promotion.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit reference to a Medical Student Elective Program application route — Tier 1 USCE.

claims.claimText: MSK lists a Clinical Undergraduate Research Experience (CURE) under training programs.
claims.limitations: 'Undergraduate' likely indicates pre-medical/undergraduate students rather than medical students; eligibility not explicit on this page. UNKNOWN_SCOPE.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Research experience program named alongside medical-student opportunities; pre-residency research lane.

futureLaneSignals.quote:
Bone Marrow Transplant Fellowship
We offer a one-year fellowship program in bone marrow transplant with opportunities to do clinical research.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: MSK offers a one-year Bone Marrow Transplant fellowship with clinical research opportunities.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship program explicitly described — Tier 2 trainee content.

futureLaneSignals.quote:
Hematology Fellowship
We offer a three-year fellowship training program in hematology, with rotations at nearby NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: MSK offers a three-year Hematology fellowship with rotations at nearby NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship training program explicitly described — Tier 2 trainee content.

futureLaneSignals.claimText: MSK references Residencies and Internship within its Department of Medicine training programs.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Label only; no program-level detail on this page.

futureLaneSignals.claimText: MSK has a Graduate Medical Education (GME) program.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit GME label — Tier 2 trainee content.

sourceScopeConflicts.quote: Department of Medicine: Training Opportunities | Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center

claims.quote: SKI’s research enterprise is organized into nine programs that encompass the diversity of modern biomedical sciences. SKI investigators pursue research that spans the full spectrum of discovery, from basic science to preclinical, translational, and postclinical research.
claims.claimText: SKI research enterprise organized into nine programs spanning basic to postclinical research; no audience eligibility for medical students stated.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes SKI's research enterprise at institutional level with no specific mention of medical student, IMG, or trainee access; audience unspecified.
claims.limitations: Audience eligibility for medical students or visiting trainees not stated on this page.

claims.quote: The EIO Center supports comprehensive career development program to support trainees engaged in laboratory training in the areas of immuno-oncology (IO), tumor microenvironment (TME), microbe-cancer interactions, and related areas.
claims.claimText: EIO Scholars Program supports trainees in laboratory training in immuno-oncology and related areas — audience 'trainees' unspecified.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Mentions a career development program for 'trainees' in laboratory training; the trainee audience aligns with Tier 2 (research/training) rather than Tier 1 medical-student USCE.
claims.limitations: Audience 'trainees' is not explicitly defined as medical students, residents, or fellows.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Postdoctoral researchers are a vital aspect of SKI’s research enterprise. The collaborative culture provides an exciting and stimulating training experience where interdisciplinary teamwork and learning are strongly encouraged.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: SKI offers postdoctoral training positions in its research enterprise.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Postdoctoral research positions for PhD-level researchers — Tier 3 career/training resource, not pre-residency USCE.

futureLaneSignals.quote:
Tri-Institutional MD/PhD Program
Sponsored collectively by the Sloan Kettering Institute, The Rockefeller University, and Weill Cornell Medical College, the Tri-Institutional MD/PhD Program trains physician-scientists to become leaders in biomedical research.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Tri-Institutional MD/PhD Program co-sponsored by SKI, Rockefeller, and Weill Cornell — physician-scientist training.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: MD/PhD program is structured graduate medical education — Tier 2 trainee context, not pre-residency USCE.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The Human Oncology and Pathogenesis Program (HOPP) within Memorial Hospital also offers an eight-week summer experience for high school students interested in pursuing a career in biomedical sciences.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: HOPP offers an eight-week summer research experience for high school students.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: High school summer research program — outside the medical-student USCE pipeline; flagged for internal review only.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Audience is high school students, not medical students or IMGs.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The Mechanistic Biology Summer Program gives approximately 10 outstanding undergraduate freshmen, sophomores, and juniors the opportunity to train in SKI laboratories.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Mechanistic Biology Summer Program trains ~10 undergraduate freshmen, sophomores, and juniors in SKI labs.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Undergraduate summer research — not within the medical-student or physician pipeline; not USCE.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Audience is undergraduate freshmen/sophomores/juniors, not medical students.

futureLaneSignals.claimText: Site lists open faculty and postdoctoral positions navigation.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Navigation references faculty and postdoctoral open positions — Tier 3 career resource.
futureLaneSignals.limitations: Only a navigation list; no job specifics on this page.
